title: The High-Resolution Challenge

options.text: Generating large images directly is unstable because the Discriminator spots flaws too easily.
Large images require less memory and are easier to generate.
The Discriminator gets confused by large images and stops working.

exercises.explain: Generating a large, detailed image right from the start is too hard because the Discriminator can spot tiny errors instantly, causing training to fail.
exercises.prompt: Why is it difficult to train a standard GAN to generate high-resolution images like 1024x1024 directly?

cards.body: Imagine trying to draw a hyper-realistic masterpiece on a giant canvas on your first day of art school. That's what we ask a standard GAN to do when we demand a high-resolution image right away!
cards.title: Too Big, Too Soon

cards.body: The High-Resolution Challenge refers to the extreme instability in GAN training when trying to generate large images (like 1024x1024 pixels) directly. The discriminator easily spots tiny local imperfections in the massive pixel grid, making it impossible for the generator to learn.
cards.title: The High-Resolution Challenge

cards.body: Think of a beginner learning to play piano. If you force them to play a complex, fast concerto on day one, they will fail completely because the teacher (discriminator) will point out a million mistakes. It's much better to start with simple three-note melodies.
cards.title: Learning to Play Piano
The Pixel Pitfall
cards.body: If a Generator attempts to create a 1024x1024 face, it might place a few pixels slightly off-color. Because the image is so large, the Discriminator instantly detects this unnatural pixel pattern and rejects it, giving the Generator no useful feedback on how to improve the overall shape.

You might think that simply using a bigger computer or adding more layers solves high-resolution generation. But it doesn't! The issue is the training balance: the discriminator becomes too powerful too quickly when looking at large grids.
cards.title: Myth: Just Add More Layers

left.text: Easily spots tiny pixel errors in a giant 1024x1024 grid, halting progress.

right.text: Gets overwhelmed trying to coordinate millions of pixels all at once.

exercises.prompt: Why does directly generating high-resolution images cause GAN training to collapse?
exercises.explain: When the image size is large, the discriminator has an easy time finding flaws, which overpowers the generator and stops it from learning.

options.text: The discriminator easily finds flaws in large grids, overwhelming the generator.
The generator runs out of random noise to create large images.
Large images make the discriminator too slow to make decisions.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Generating a 1024x1024 image directly from noise is highly unstable for a standard GAN.
exercises.explain: True! The massive amount of detail in a 1024x1024 grid makes it easy for the discriminator to win the game immediately, halting training.

exercises.sentence: Generating massive pixel grids directly causes training [blank] because the discriminator easily spots simple flaws.
exercises.prompt: Generating massive pixel grids directly causes training ____ because the discriminator easily spots simple flaws.
exercises.explain: Directly scaling up the target resolution leads to training instability because the discriminator quickly dominates the generator.

exercises.prompt: Which of these looks like the result of a GAN failing the High-Resolution Challenge?
exercises.explain: The noisy, disorganized artifact patterns in a high-res image show a GAN that failed to coordinate its pixels, a classic symptom of high-resolution training collapse.

options.text: A 1024x1024 image filled with chaotic, noisy checkerboard patterns instead of a face.
A perfectly clear, studio-quality portrait of a person.

exercises.explain: To solve this challenge, we must find a way to grow the image size step-by-step. In the next lesson, we will look at Progressive Growing, which starts small and gradually increases resolution!
exercises.prompt: How can we overcome the instability of generating high-resolution images directly?

options.text: By starting with small images and gradually adding layers for higher resolutions.
By deleting the discriminator and training only the generator.
By using lower quality training photos.

concept: Generating high-resolution images (e.g. 1024x1024) directly is highly unstable because the discriminator easily spots simple flaws in large grids.
